title: chore(enter-billing): delete dead hash.ts, getEstimatedPrice, collapse CheckoutCohort wrapper
author: voodoohop
pr_body_excerpt:
- Delete `utils/hash.ts` entirely (dead: `bufferToHex`/`simpleHash`/`createCacheKey`, zero callers)
- Remove dead `getEstimatedPrice` from model-stats.ts
- Collapse the over-built `CheckoutCohort` wrapper to a plain `CohortId` string (analytics-only)

Verified non-breaking: biome clean, affected tests green vs the main baseline. Net −88 lines.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

gist.headline: Trimming the dead branches
gist.blurb: We swept out some unused code and simplified our billing analytics wrappers. A lighter codebase means a happier hive!
gist.summary: Removed unused hashing utilities (hash.ts) and simplified the CheckoutCohort analytics wrapper to a plain string in the enter-billing service.
gist.impact: This internal cleanup reduces codebase complexity by 88 lines without affecting any user-facing billing functionality.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee and the Nomnom creature cleaning up a digital garden. Nomnom is eating deprecated code snippets (represented as dry leaves or old paper), while Polly sweeps up.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan Nomnom creature happily eating dry, brown pixelated leaves labeled 'dead code' while Polly the bee mascot sweeps a digital garden path with a tiny broom. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and warm peach gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
